question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getSharedListener()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getSharedListener() does not check whether the topicId in the filter corresponds to an existing topic before creating a Redis subscription, causing a subscription to a non-existent topic channel that may later receive injected messages, allowing an attacker to pre-position a listener for a topic that does not yet exist?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the subscribe() Flux does not enforce message ordering by consensusTimestamp, so that if the Redis pub/sub channel delivers messages out of order (e.g., due to network jitter or pipeline reordering), the subscriber receives them in the wrong order and observes a reorganized transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the subscribe() Flux does not enforce that sequenceNumber is strictly greater than the previously received sequenceNumber, so that a message with a lower sequenceNumber than the last received one passes through and causes the subscriber to observe a history that appears to go backwards?
